data.title: American Express in charts: Q3 network volumes +8.7%, provisions for credit losses -5% Y/Y
European defense stocks sink as Trump sets another meeting with Putin
data.description:
European defense stocks tumbled Friday after President Trump and Russian counterpart Putin agreed to another summit meeting to discuss the war in Ukraine, possibly within the next two weeks in Budapest.

The sector had hit record highs during the week as the European Union proposed a reform of its military planning and procurement, aimed at making the bloc ready for combat by the end of the decade, as relations with Russia continue to deteriorate.

In European trading: Germany's Rheinmetall (OTCPK:RNMBF) (OTCPK:RNMBY) -6.7%, Germany's Hensoldt (OTCPK:HAGHY) (OTCPK:HNSDF) -7.6%, Germany's Renk (OTCPK:RNKGF) -5.5%, Italy's Leonardo (OTCPK:FINMF) (OTCPK:FINMY) -4.6%, Sweden's Saab (OTCPK:SAABY) -3.5%, Norway's Kongsberg Gruppen (OTCPK:NSKFF) (OTCPK:KBGGY) -5.3%, U.K.'s BAE Systems (OTCPK:BAESF) (OTCPK:BAESY) -3%, U.K.'s Babcock (OTCPK:BCKIF) -3.5%, Spain's Indra Sistemas (OTCPK:ISMAF) (OTCPK:ISMAY) -2.7%, France's Thales (OTCPK:THLEF) (OTCPK:THLLY) -2.5%, France's Dassault (OTCPK:DUAVF) -0.8%.

Friday's moves could reverse if there is no concrete result from the summit, as it was the case after the August meeting in Alaska.

data.title: Webster Financial GAAP EPS of $1.54 beats by $0.01, revenue of $732.6M beats by $3.96M
data.description:
• Webster Financial press release (NYSE:WBS): Q3 GAAP EPS of $1.54 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $732.6M (+13.1% Y/Y) beats by $3.96M.
  • Period end loans and leases balance of $55.1 billion, up $1.4 billion, or 2.6 percent from prior quarter.
  • Period end deposits balance of $68.2 billion, up $1.9 billion, or 2.8 percent, from prior quarter.
  • Provision for credit losses of $44.0 million.
  • Return on average assets of 1.27 percent.
  • Return on average tangible common equity of 17.64 percent1.
  • Net interest margin of 3.40 percent, down 4 basis points from prior quarter.
  • Common equity tier 1 ratio of 11.40 percent.
  • Efficiency ratio of 45.79 percent.
  • Tangible common equity ratio of 7.50 percent.

data.title: Prospect Ridge announces $3M flow-through private placement
data.description:
• Prospect Ridge Resources (OTCQB:PRRSF) has announced a non-brokered private placement of up to $3M via issuance of up to 25M critical mineral flow-through units priced at $0.12 per unit.
  • Each unit includes one flow-through common share and one-half of a warrant; full warrants are exercisable at $0.18 for two years.
  • Proceeds will fund eligible Canadian exploration expenses, primarily a 2,000-meter drill program at the Camelot project near Horsefly, B.C.

data.title: Ally Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $1.15 beats by $0.14, revenue of $2.16B beats by $50M
data.description:
• Ally Financial press release (NYSE:ALLY): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.15 beats by $0.14.
  • Revenue of $2.16B (+3.3% Y/Y) beats by $50M.
  • NIM ex. OID1 of 3.55% is up 10 bps quarter over quarter
  • Common equity tier 1 ratio of 10.1% increased ~20 bps quarter over quarter | Fully phased-in AOCI CET1 of 8.0% up ~90 bps since year-end 2024

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: American Express tops Q3 estimates; provisions for credit losses were $1.3B
VinFast Auto sets an EV sales milestone in Vietnam
data.description:
VinFast Auto Ltd. (NASDAQ:VFS) announced on Friday that it reached the milestone of selling 100,000 electric vehicles in Vietnam within just nine months of the year.

Through the end of September, VinFast (NASDAQ:VFS) had delivered 103,884 EVs year-to-date to set a national record. The best-selling model was the compact VF 3, which alone accounted for 31,386 units in the first nine months of 2025. The VF 5 (30,956 units) and VF 6 (14,425 units) also contributed significantly to the company’s domestic success. VinFast's (VFS) strategy of offering affordable, compact, and mass-market-friendly models was highlighted as being particularly effective in driving EV adoption in Vietnam.

VinFast Auto (VFS) launched its first electric vehicles for the Vietnamese market in the early 2020s, quickly ascending to the top of the sales charts. By 2025, VinFast had achieved 11 consecutive months as Vietnam’s best-selling automaker, surpassing both local and international competitors. 

Shares of VinFast Auto (VFS) were down 0.9% in premarket trading. The EV stock is off 18.4% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Truist Financial Q3 earnings beat; targets 15% ROTCE in '27
data.description:
Truist Financial (NYSE:TFC) stock climbed 2.3% in Friday premarket trading after the bank set a return on tangible common equity target of 15% for 2027 following Q3 beats on earnings and revenue. The results reflected a rebound in investment banking activity and trading and growth in wealth management fees.

The bank reaffirmed 2025 guidance for adjusted revenue growth of 1.5%-2.5% and adjusted expense growth of ~1%. 

For Q4, it expects adjusted revenue to grow 1%-2% from Q3’s $5.24B, implying $5.29B-$5.34B (midpoint $5.32B vs. $5.30B consensus). Adjusted expenses are projected to be stable with from Q3’s $3.0B.

The company said it also expects to reprice ~$11B of fixed rate loans and $3B of investment securities during Q4. Its investment portfolio runoff may be used to fund loan growth.

The lender expects to buy back up to $750M of its shares in Q4, vs. the $500M it repurchased in Q3.

Looking further ahead, it expects growth in adjusted revenue, adjusted EPS growth, and stock buybacks to accelerate in 2026. Truist (NYSE:TFC) targets 15% return on tangible common equity in 2027 vs. 13.6% reported in Q3 2025.

Truist (NYSE:TFC) also sees increased stock buybacks, targeting 10% CET1 ratio by Dec. 31, 2027.

Q2 adjusted EPS of $1.04, vs. the $0.99 consensus, rose from $0.91 in Q2 and $0.97 in last year’s Q3.

Q2 adjusted revenue of $5.24B, topping the $5.20B consensus, grew from $5.04B in the prior quarter and $5.14B a year ago.

Chairman and CEO bill Rogers said the Q3 earnings were "underscored by robust fee income growth in investment banking and trading and wealth, healthy loan expansion, and continued expense and credit discipline."

Net interest income (“TE”) of $3.68B, missing the Visible Alpha consensus of $3.71B, increased from $3.64B in Q2 and $3.66B in last year’s Q3.

Noninterest income of $1.56B (vs. the Visible Alpha estimate of $1.49B) rose from $1.40B in the prior quarter and $1.48B a year ago. The Q/Q increase reflected higher investment banking and trading income and wealth management income.

Noninterest expense of $3.01B edged up from $2.99B in Q2 and $2.93B a year ago.

Provision for credit losses was $436M, lower than the $480M Visible Alpha estimate, declined from $488M in Q2 and $448M in Q3 2024.

Truist’s (TFC) adjusted preprovision net revenue of $2.25B increased from $2.10B in Q2 and $2.22B in Q3 2024.

Average total deposits of $397B fell from $400B in Q2. Average total loans and leases were $322B vs. $313B in the prior quarter.

Conference call at 8:00 AM ET.

Earlier, Truist Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $1.04 beats by $0.05, revenue of $5.24B beats by $40M

data.title: Achieve, Disc, Revolution among winners of initial FDA national priority vouchers
data.description:
Achieve Life Sciences (NASDAQ:ACHV), Disc Medicine (NASDAQ:IRON), and Revolution Medicines (NASDAQ:RVMD) have been selected among the first nine recipients of the FDA Commissioner’s National Priority Review vouchers, which aim to speed up review timelines of nationally important medicines.  

Achieve Life Sciences (NASDAQ:ACHV) and Disc Medicine (NASDAQ:IRON), whose experimental therapies, Cytisinicline and Bitopertin, for nicotine vaping addiction and porphyria, respectively, were selected gained ~25% and ~20% in the premarket on Friday, after the news.

Revolution Medicines (NASDAQ:RVMD) added ~9% after receiving a voucher thanks to its pancreatic cancer therapy RMC-6236.

Other listed drugmakers to receive new priority review vouchers include Regeneron (NASDAQ:REGN), Sanofi (SNY), and the EMD Serono unit of Merck KGaA (OTCPK:MKGAF), (OTCPK:MKKGY).

The FDA unveiled the pilot program in June in an attempt to shorten the drug review timeline from the standard 10–12-month period to 1–2 months from the filing of a marketing application.

Sponsors of products designed to address a significant national priority, such as a major unmet medical need and domestic drug manufacturing, were eligible to receive the vouchers.

During an event held at the White House on Thursday to improve access to fertility care in the U.S., President Donald Trump welcomed the issuance of a national priority voucher to EMD Serono’s fertility treatment Pergoveris.

"This drug would directly compete against a much more expensive option that currently has a monopoly in the American market, and this will bring down costs very significantly,” Trump said.

data.title: Boston Scientific to acquire Nalu Medical in $533M deal
data.description:
• Boston Scientific (NYSE:BSX) will acquire Nalu Medical for $533M in cash for the equity it does not already own.

  • Nalu specializes in peripheral nerve stimulation devices for treating chronic pain in areas like the shoulder, lower back, and knee.

  • Boston Scientific has been a strategic investor in Nalu since 2017.

  • The acquisition strengthens BSX’s pain management portfolio, complementing existing therapies like spinal cord stimulation and radiofrequency ablation.

  • Deal expected to close in H1 2026, with Nalu forecasted to exceed $60M in 2025 revenue and grow 25%+ in 2026.

  • Financial impact: Expected to be immaterial to adjusted EPS in 2026, slightly accretive in 2027, and increasingly accretive thereafter; more dilutive on GAAP basis due to amortization and related charges.
